name: Energy Sector (Global)
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **ENERGY SECTOR DIGESTS BRENT COLLAPSE TO $107.96 + GOLDMAN Q2 CUT TO $90 — XLE +0.1% TO $59.45 (FLAT 1D, -0.4% 1M, +12.4% 3M); EXXON +0.8% TO $154.88 (-5.2% 1M, +4.6% 3M).** Mixed picture: equity sector held up despite Brent -1.7% on the diplomatic-track pricing (would normally see -2 to -3% sector decline on $4 Brent move). Cheniere LNG $269.52 (-1.2% 1D, -5.1% 1M, +26.5% 3M) — softer on TTF eased to €46.93 (-2.5% May 5). RBOB Gasoline -5.2% 1D leads energy-complex decline on demand-destruction premium unwind ahead of Memorial Day driving season; Heating Oil -10.8% 1M / -0.9% 1D. WTI Crude $100.37 (-1.9% 1D, -11.1% 1M). For deployment thesis: US energy sector remains the cleanest beneficiary trade architecturally — pays WTI, sells Brent, exports LNG at TTF premium; structural Hormuz blockade keeps margins high. The 'energy-vs-Brent divergence resolves within 21 days' watching trigger is partially active — sector held up better than crude on May 5-6, suggesting equity is pricing the structural premium not just spot. Goldman's Q2 $90 forecast is conditional on diplomatic track holding; if pause framework breaks, energy reprices up sharply.
